mnemonics: FACTS: Financial, Activity, Customer, Timeliness, Satisfaction
keyPoints: Business metrics are quantifiable measures used to track and assess the status of a specific business process.
They are often derived from fact tables in a data warehouse schema and are numerical values that can be aggregated.
Key metrics are aligned with strategic business objectives (KPIs) to evaluate performance.
commonMistakes: Confusing metrics with dimensions; metrics are numbers you sum/average, dimensions are attributes you group by (like date or product).
Selecting vanity metrics that look good but don't provide actionable insight into business performance.
Failing to ensure data quality and consistency, leading to inaccurate or misleading metric calculations.
